tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function with possible nephrotic-range proteinuria and tubular dysfunction. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying renal or hepatic pathology. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, possibly due to analytical or clinical factors (e.g., hemolysis, recent blood loss). Recommend further nephrological and diabetic evaluation, and assessment for hepatic dysfunction.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
